summary: DVLT remains the book's biggest risk and it just got worse: a fresh 6/29 8-K shows the company spent $50M cash on 837 Bitcoin (via Scilex) while still running an active ATM and an unresolved CyberCatch acquisition LOI - diluting-and-speculating, not funding the core business.

UUUU.note: $300M shelf + active ATM, ~+20% YoY share count (unchanged read from last cycle). New this cycle: definitive $1.9B VAC acquisition (6/23, $718M cash + 65.85M new shares) and a $725M OSC conditional debt commitment (6/18) - the debt facility is a mild positive, reduces near-term reliance on further ATM drips, but the 65.85M-share stock component of VAC is itself a large dilution event baked into the deal.

DVLT.note: Serial diluter (855.6M+ shares out), $1B shelf, active Maxim ATM, non-binding $2B financing term sheet, CyberCatch acquisition LOI still unresolved since exclusivity lapsed ~6/15. NEW: 6/29 8-K discloses a $50M purchase of 837 Bitcoin via Scilex - burning cash (raised via dilutive offerings) on speculative crypto rather than core business, on top of the 7/9 'Mandela Digital'/stablecoin hype partnership and a 7/13 auditor change to CBIZ CPAs. Pattern is worsening, not resolving. Already on the bot's own avoid list.

CAI.note: No change: lockup passed, buyback anti-dilutive, no new secondary/shelf activity found.

AVO.note: No change: Calavo Growers acquisition closed 5/28/26 and is fully priced in; no forward dilution overhang remains. Intel.json is still flagging the Mission Produce/Calavo story as an unconfirmed 'search-engine mixup' - that read is stale, this is a closed, confirmed deal.

NTSK.note: No change: held position resolves to Netskope Inc (NASDAQ:NTSK), a $5B cybersecurity company, not NetSol (NTWK). Fundamentally sound, just off the small-cap thesis.

flags.why: Radar top-2 candidate (5.11) but this is a distressed reverse-merger pop: definitive Serapha Bio merger agreement (6/23) leaves pre-merger holders only ~3.7% of combined 'Serapha Bio' (Nasdaq: AATD), own oncology pipeline halted, 75% workforce cut, plus a 7/6 fiduciary-duty investigation into the board. Today's reported +61.7% premarket move is sell-the-news risk on a stock in wind-down, not a buyable catalyst. No change this cycle - still avoid.

Top radar candidate (5.93) but carries an active $100M ATM since 2024 plus a Feb-2026 $100M offering (20M shares at $5) now underwater at $3.75 - dilution machinery is live even though no new prospectus filed this cycle. BLA rolling submission (module 1, 6/29) is real progress, but doesn't remove the financing overhang on a still-cash-hungry clinical-stage biotech.

Automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 1/13/26, already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM - ongoing drip-dilution setup. Currently below buy threshold (3.33) so moot for now; flag if score rises.

dilution_watch.note: NEW: $50M purchase of 837 Bitcoin via Scilex - diluted/raised cash being deployed into speculative crypto rather than core business.
Non-binding $2.0B structured-financing term sheet (four $500M tranches at $1.55-2.00/share); full draw implies >50% dilution and possible board-control shift.
Maxim Group ATM agreement plus 2.73M warrants at $0.6325 strike - active drip-dilution mechanism on top of the $1B shelf.
Binding LOI to acquire CyberCatch Holdings for ~49.9M new shares; 45-day exclusivity lapsed ~6/15 with still no confirmed definitive agreement or lapse announcement - status remains unresolved.
$100M underwritten offering, 20M shares at $5.00, now underwater at $3.75; $100M ATM (since 2024) remains active. No new filing found this cycle.
Definitive Serapha Bio merger plus $230M concurrent private placement; existing holders diluted to ~3.7% pro forma.
Automatic shelf effective, used twice via 424B5; $50M ATM active.
Definitive $1.9B VAC (Vacuumschmelze) acquisition: $718M cash + 65.853M new shares issued - large one-time dilution event tied to the deal, separate from the existing $300M ATM shelf.

disagreements: intel.json again carries no alert on DVLT this snapshot despite a worsening picture (new $50M Bitcoin purchase disclosed 6/29) - this remains a held critical-risk position and deserves a standing warning every cycle.

intel.json (17:25Z) still frames the AVO/Calavo story as an unconfirmed 'search-engine mixup' - the acquisition closed 5/28/26 (confirmed via Mission Produce IR + SEC filings); no forward risk remains, the open question is just stale sourcing on intel's side.
intel.json flags NTSK ticker ambiguity as unresolved again - the held position is confirmed to be Netskope Inc (NASDAQ:NTSK), not NetSol.
